title: Applied Researcher
reason: Strong Python/CV/ML background with policy training and debugging experience, but lacks direct robotics, control, hardware, manipulation, or real-world robot deployment experience central to this role.

title: Build & Release Engineer
reason: The candidate has strong Python/C++ engineering and some Docker/Linux/backend automation experience, but lacks direct senior build/release ownership, GitHub CI/CD at scale, Bazel, Kubernetes, Terraform, and platform infrastructure depth required for this role.

title: Controls Engineer
reason: Strong Python/C++ and ML/CV background with some real-time automation and learned-policy exposure, but the resume lacks core controls depth in PID/LQR/MPC/inverse dynamics, robotics hardware bring-up, embedded protocols, and electromechanical debugging required for this role.

title: Deployments Software Engineer
reason: Strong Python/C++ and computer vision/ML background with some performance optimization and screen-based automation fit parts of the role, but the resume lacks direct robotics teleoperation, low-latency streaming/video, networking, real-time systems, or production distributed debugging experience central to this posting.

title: Electrical Engineer (UMI)
reason: The candidate has strong computer vision and software/ML experience but lacks the core electrical engineering, PCB design, high-speed hardware, RF, power management, and board bring-up background required for this role.

title: Member of Technical Staff - Product Engineering
ML Infra Engineer
reason: Strong ML/CV and Python/PyTorch background with some infrastructure and optimization experience, but limited evidence of JAX, distributed large-scale training, GPU/TPU orchestration, Kubernetes/SLURM, or deep ML systems work required for this role.

title: Research Scientist
reason: Strong ML/CV implementation background with PyTorch, C++, system-building, and some research-adjacent publications, but the resume lacks robotics experience and a clear record of fundamental research advances expected for a Research Scientist role at Physical Intelligence.

title: Robot Operator
reason: The candidate has strong AI/CV and automation background relevant to robotics data quality and annotation, but this is primarily a hands-on, repetitive robot teleoperation role requiring manual dexterity and shift-based data collection rather than an engineering role matching his seniority.

title: Robot Prototype Technician
reason: The candidate has strong software, computer vision, and some robotics-adjacent AI experience, but the role is primarily hands-on electromechanical prototyping, fabrication, soldering, CAD, diagnostics, and repair, which are largely absent from the resume.

title: Robotics Software Engineer
reason: Strong CV/ML, Python/C++ and some real-time image-processing background are relevant, but the role is mainly low-level runtime systems, Linux/kernel, drivers, networking, video streaming, and robotics production latency work, which the resume only partially supports.
